title: For Thirty Years, Medicine Told Women With Hot Flashes to Take Hormones or Suffer. That Just Changed.
description: Two newly approved drugs — fezolinetant and elinzanetant — work through a completely different mechanism than estrogen, targeting the hypothalamic neurons that actually trigger hot flashes. For the estimated 20–25% of women who cannot take hormone therapy due to cancer history or cardiovascular risk, this is the first genuinely new option in over three decades. Here's what the mechanism reveals about why hot flashes happen at all — and what these drugs can and cannot do.
heroImageAlt: Editorial illustration for For Thirty Years, Medicine Told Women With Hot Flashes to Take Hormones or Suffer. That Just Changed.
